resources.string: A comprehensive solution to decrypt and export Happ-only proxy subscriptions
HWID is unknown. Enter manually\nor launch Happ
Nothing to show. Launch Happ and import/update encrypted subscriptions. Decrypted links will appear here.

Fill in the three fields above and click the Profiles button—your subscription configurations will appear here, and you\'ll be able to copy them into any proxy client. The Subscription button allows you to update profiles in other apps via the Bridge service.
Forcefully replace Hardware ID in target applications with the value from Happwner

Paste the link to your Happ VPN subscription.

Supported formats:
• https://
• http://
• happ://add
• happ://crypt (including crypt5)
• http://127.0.0.1:8166 (Happwner Bridge link)

Every device has its own unique HWID. VPN providers use it to identify which device is connected to the subscription and may restrict access if the device limit is reached. By spoofing the identifier, you can bypass this restriction by entering the HWID of any device already connected to the subscription.
When requesting subscriptions, Happ sends its app name and version to the VPN provider. Happwner mimics this behavior.
An alternative way to decrypt links using Xposed/LSPatch. This may be helpful if the built-in decryptor becomes obsolete
Convert received JSON arrays into deeplinks. URI links are less feature-rich than JSON but compatible with virtually any client
Decode Base64 payloads received from the server
Convert received configs for Happ (Xray format) into configs for NekoBox, husi, Hiddify and similar clients (sing-box format)

When requesting VPN profiles Happwner converts the received Base64/JSON data into formats compatible with other apps.

Disable these functions if profiles are processed incorrectly.

Some Xray features have no equivalents in sing-box, so not every profile can be converted. Unsupported profiles will be skipped.


Transports:
• xhttp, splithttp, mekya, mkcp, meek — no equivalents in sing-box;
• tcp/raw with obfuscation — only none and http are supported;
• quic with obfuscation or headers — sing-box supports raw QUIC only.

Protocols:
• VLESS with flow xtls-rprx-direct/splice/origin — incompatible with Vision;
• VLESS with encryption ≠ none (e.g. post-quantum);
• VMess with non-standard security (supported: auto/none/zero/aes-128-gcm/chacha20-poly1305/aes-128-ctr);
• security: xtls — deprecated, replaced in modern Xray with tls + flow xtls-rprx-vision;
• Shadowsocks with non-standard method — supported: aes-*-gcm, (x)chacha20-ietf-poly1305, 2022-blake3-aes-128/256-gcm, 2022-blake3-chacha20-poly1305, aes-*-ctr/cfb, chacha20-ietf, xchacha20, rc4-md5, none;
• Shadowsocks with plugins other than obfs-local and v2ray-plugin.

Routing:
• Mux.Cool — incompatible with sing-mux;
• source_geoip — sing-box doesn\'t match geo by source IP;
• balancer.fallbackTag — urltest retries instead of switching;
• balancer.strategy: leastLoad — replaced with urltest (≈ leastPing).

DNS:
• expectIPs/unexpectedIPs — DNS rules match the request, not the response.

Routing rules:
The Xray core\'s geosite: and geoip: rules are converted into sing-box rule-sets compatible with the sing-box for Android app and the CLI core. These clients will automatically download the required domain and IP lists from SagerNet repositories.

Note that other sing-box clients (Hiddify, husi, NekoBox, Karing, etc.) usually ignore the routing (rule-set) shipped with imported profiles and use rules configured by the user through the UI.
Unlock export and editing of profiles inside encrypted subscriptions
Run a local HTTP proxy server to update Happ subscriptions in other apps
Ignore power consumption limits. Recommended for stable server operation; required for Watchdog
Force awakening of the server process every 10 seconds via system timers. Helper service
Allows updating Happ subscriptions in other apps
\n\n… [Text truncated to save resources. Press \"Copy\" to get the full result]

This feature allows updating Happ subscriptions in any proxy client through the Happwner service. After clicking \"OK\", Happwner will request permission to display notifications, start the service, and copy the link to the clipboard—import it into your app.

Important: enable \"Bypass LAN\" or add a geoip:private rule to direct, otherwise the update will not work when your proxy client\'s connection is active.

This feature allows updating Happ subscriptions in any proxy client through the Happwner service.

Important: enable \"Bypass LAN\" or add a geoip:private rule to direct, otherwise the update will not work when your proxy client\'s connection is active.

A free and open-source tool for exporting encrypted Happ subscriptions to any apps.

Status: %2$s

Version: %1$s
Developers: Omegaplex, slavrom21
Repository: GitHub
Dynamic color from home screen wallpaper
